label: Formal verification of models

node.label: Formal verification of models
node.definition: Formal model verification proves that a model satisfies a specified property over an input region or abstraction, such as robustness, bounded output, or monotonicity.
node.why_it_matters: It can replace sampled evidence with mathematical guarantees for narrowly defined high-consequence properties.
limitations: Verification scales poorly with model size and nonlinear complexity, and the specified property may omit real hazards.

dependencies.rationale: Formal verification of models measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Formal methods, specifications & proof obligations.
Formal verification of models checks candidate behavior or artifacts against evidence or constraints from Constraint satisfaction, SAT & SMT solving.
Formal verification of models measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Robustness, distribution shift & OOD evaluation.
Formal verification of models measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Ablation, interventions & causal analysis.
